title: astragalus geniculatus
intro: Astragalus geniculatus, also known as the bent milkvetch or bent tick clover, is a perennial legume native to the mountainous regions of East Asia, characterized by its unique bent or curved stems and clusters of pink or purple flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, primarily due to its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which allow it to support the body's natural response to stress and promote overall well-being. The medicinal properties of Astragalus geniculatus have been found to have various health benefits, including enhancing immune function, reducing fatigue, and improving cardiovascular health. Astragalus geniculatus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and can also be consumed as a supplement in powder or extract form.
